analysis: Looking at your data, I only have one night here — the early hours of 17 June — not seven nights. That night runs from just after midnight to about 3:45am, giving you roughly 3 hours 45 minutes of sleep total, which is a short one. Your REM was actually decent for the duration, showing up in solid chunks around 1:16am, 2:47am, and 3:26am, which means your brain was doing the good stuff even in a compressed window. The gap is simply the hours — whatever the stack of interventions is doing, that night needed more time in bed to work with. Give the routine more runway and it has somewhere to go.
